raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 2639 aims to prevent student suicide, which is the second leading cause of death among 10- to 24-year-olds.",
    "Having outdated suicide prevention policies does little to protect students.",
    "The bill requires schools to review their suicide prevention policies at least every five years and update them if necessary.",
    "Assembly Bill 2639 builds upon Assembly Bill 2246, which requires schools serving grades 7 to 12 to adopt a suicide prevention policy in consultation with various stakeholders.",
    "Teachers and school staff play a crucial role in preventing suicide and helping students in crisis.",
    "Students spend roughly 1,000 hours per year in school.",
    "In a national survey by the Jason Foundation, students reported that a teacher was the person they would turn to for help regarding a friend who might be suicidal.",
    "The passage of AB 2639 aims to ensure that suicide prevention policies across California's school districts are regularly reviewed."
  ]
}
```
